name: Iddict

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decode an id-dict from a JSON value.

values.name: diff
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key does not appear in the second id-dict.
values.type: Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty id-dict.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode an id-dict to a JSON value.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Keep only the key-value pairs that pass the given test.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in an id-dict from lowest key to highest key.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b -> b) -> b -> Iddict.Iddict a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in an id-dict from highest key to lowest key.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b -> b) -> b -> Iddict.Iddict a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert an association list into an id-dict.

values.name: get
values.comment: Get a value from the id-dict using its key.

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a new value into the id-dict. Given that the id-dict generates its
key, the function returns both the updated id-dict as the newly generated key.

    x = empty |> insert "hello" -- ( 0, <Iddict with value "hello"> )

    case x of
        ( _, id-dict ) ->
            get 0 id-dict -- Just "hello"
values.type: a -> Iddict.Iddict a -> ( Basics.Int, Iddict.Iddict a )

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Keep a key-value pair when its key appears in the second id-dict.
Preference is given to values in the first id-dict.
values.type: Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Determine if an id-dict is empty.

values.name: keys
values.comment: Get all of the keys from the id-dict, sorted from lowest to highest.

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to all values in an id-dict.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a key is in an id-dict.

values.name: merge
values.comment:
The most general way of combining two id-dicts. You provide three
accumulators for when a given key appears:

    1. Only in the left id-dict.
    2. In both id-dicts.
    3. Only in the right id-dict.

You then traverse all the keys from lowest to highest, building up whatever you
want.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> result -> result) -> (Basics.Int -> a -> b -> result -> result) -> (Basics.Int -> b -> result -> result) -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict b -> result -> result

values.name: partition
values.comment:
Partition an id-dict according to some test. The first id-dict contains all
key-value pairs which passed the test, and the second contains the pairs that
did not.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> Iddict.Iddict a -> ( Iddict.Iddict a, Iddict.Iddict a )

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove a key-value pair from the id-dict. If the key is not found, no
changes are made.

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create an id-dict with a single value.

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of key-value pairs in the id-dict.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert an id-dict into an association list of key-value pairs, sorted by keys.

values.name: union
values.comment:
Combine two id-dicts. If there is a collision, preference is given to the
first id-dict.
values.type: Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update the value of an id-dict for a specific key with a given function.
This is also the only option that allows you to insert values at a specific key,
so be careful with this feature!
values.type: Basics.Int -> (Maybe.Maybe a -> Maybe.Maybe a) -> Iddict.Iddict a -> Iddict.Iddict a

values.name: values
values.comment: Get all of the values from an id-dict, in the order of their keys.
